title: Local recovery priorities meeting
summary: A calm meeting format for deciding what to repair, clean, share, or ask for first.
content: Recovery can become chaotic if every person pushes a different urgent task. A priorities meeting uses one list: life and health, water, sanitation, shelter, food, communication, access, records, and comfort. Keep meetings short, factual, and recorded. Good recovery meetings protect attention. Start with confirmed facts, then list blockers, then assign only a few tasks that can be finished before the next review. Do not let the loudest person define every priority. Keep unresolved items visible instead of arguing them repeatedly.
materials: Dry storage. Context: “Local recovery priorities meeting”.
Basic hand tools if relevant — for «Local recovery priorities meeting»
steps: Write what cannot be solved locally.
End with the next meeting time.
warnings: Do not let the loudest person become the only priority.
Do not ignore sanitation because repairs feel more visible.
common_mistakes: Working from memory only for Local recovery priorities meeting.
Letting one person control the whole system for Local recovery priorities meeting.
Skipping records because the task feels simple for Local recovery priorities meeting.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Local recovery priorities meeting with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Local recovery priorities meeting if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Local recovery priorities meeting and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Local recovery priorities meeting or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Local recovery priorities meeting are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores food safety, dry storage, clean tools, pests, and water use and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Local recovery priorities meeting”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Local recovery priorities meeting as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Local recovery priorities meeting when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Local recovery priorities meeting.
short_term: For the first hours of Local recovery priorities meeting, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Local recovery priorities meeting remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Local recovery priorities meeting fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Local recovery priorities meeting to the real environment: check food safety, dry storage, clean tools, pests, and water use, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
